files.00_Overview.md: This file lacks 3W Framework. Add: 'What is Professional Practice?' section before Key Areas. Add Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways at the end. The bilingual headers are redundant - use consistent format.
files.01_Project_Organization.md: Missing Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways. No clear 'Why' section explaining the importance of organization. Add practical examples of BAD vs GOOD organization patterns. Quick Reference is good but needs 'Key Takeaways' summary.
files.02_Documentation_Standards.md: Good structure but missing Learning Objectives. 'Why' section exists but could be stronger. Add Key Takeaways. Case documentation template is good - expand with validation-specific examples. Add anti-patterns section (common documentation mistakes).
files.03_Open_Source_Contribution.md: BEST file in folder - excellent 3W Framework, clear Learning Objectives, comprehensive content. Only minor issue: inconsistent formatting with other files (different header style). Add Key Takeaways section at end to complete it.
files.04_Version_Control_Git.md: Missing Learning Objectives entirely. No 'Why' section explaining Git benefits. Good Quick Reference but lacks Key Takeaways. .gitignore section is OpenFOAM-specific (good) but needs more explanation. Add practical Git workflow examples with OpenFOAM cases.
files.05_Testing_and_QA.md: EXCEPTIONAL - complete 3W Framework with clear Learning Objectives, strong 'Why' with Real-world Impact warning. Comprehensive content covering Validation/Verification, GCI, Automated Testing. Only minor: add Key Takeaways summary section.

global_strategy: Apply 3W Framework (What/Why/How) consistently across all files. Standardize bilingual formatting (current mix creates inconsistency). Ensure ALL files have: (1) Clear Learning Objectives at start, (2) 'What/Why/How' structure in body, (3) Key Takeaways summary at end. Files 03 and 05 demonstrate the target quality level - use them as templates. Consider consolidating related content: 01-02 form a natural pair (organization+documentation), 04-05 form another pair (version control+testing). 03 stands alone well as advanced topic.
